def: s.m. A weight equal to four māshās; a sword, scimitar; a sacrificial knife; a hatchet; a stone-cutter's chisel; a spade, hoe; borax; a slope; a kind of elephant-apple or wood-apple; a weight of silver put for a coin
n. f. 1. Share; portion. 2. Appraisement; valuation. 3. (Hin. prescriptions ). Four māshās .
s.m. A pin, a rivet; a stitch; a join; solder; a needle-full of thread (or as much as would be used in one sewing); — a tub made of stones
more: s. m. 1. A stitch. 2. A tub made of stones. 3. Solder. ٿانکي لگانا ṭānke lagānā, 1. To stitch. 2. To solder.
subs: ṭāṅkā phernā (-meṅ), To put a stitch (in), pass a stitch (through)
ṭāṅkā lagānā (-meṅ), To put a stitch, or a rivet, &c. (in); see ṭāṅke lagānā
(-ke), Stitches to come open or undone; rivets or fastenings to become loosened; (fig.) to be unhinged, be undone, ruined, or destroyed
(-ke), Stitches to open out or be undone; (fig.) to have one's secrets, or designs, &c. exposed or betrayed
(-meṅ), To put some stitches (in), to stitch (a garment, or a wound, &c.); to rivet; to solder; to join.

def: s.m. Jingle, clink, ring (of plates, dishes, &c. = ṭanak, q.v.). s.f. A harsh sound; jingle, tinkle, ring (of metal, china, &c.); throb, beat, shooting pain; spirit, mettle.
more: s. m. The jingle (of plates, dishes, &c.).

def: n. m. 1. A stitch. 2. A join; solder; cement. 1. Sone kī aṅgūṭhī, pītal kā ṭāṅkā; māṅ chhināl, pūt bāṅkā! Prov. A ring of gold patched up with brass; the mother a bad one, the son for a dandy must pass! 2. Dillī ke bāṅke, jinkī jūtī meṅ sau sau ṭāṅke . Prov. A Delhi blade, his shoes with a hundred patches overlaid! ṭāṅke udhaṛnā , v. n. 1. To be opened or unripped (the stitches). Ishq meṅ ādmī ke ṭāṅke udhaṛte haiṅ . Prov. Love rips open every joint of the body. 2. To be destroyed; ruined; undone. ṭāṅkā-pānā , n. m. Friendship; amity. ṭāṅke khulnā , v. n. lit . To be ripped open (the stitches). To be betrayed (secrets, designs). ṭāṅkā lagānā, yā denā , v. a. 1. ( ṭāṅkā bharnā ) To stitch; sew. 2. To join; solder.
s.f. = ṭāṅk, q.v. s.m. A weight of four māshās; — s.f. An iron pin; a rivet; a stitch; part, portion, share; the parts of a bow; — assessment; appraisement, valuation.
(see ٿنکار) v. a. (dakh.) To ring, to jingle.
s.m. The mulberry, Morus indica.
s.f. = ṭaṅk, q.v. s.m. A weight of four māshās; — s.f. An iron pin; a rivet; a stitch; part, portion, share; the parts of a bow; — assessment; appraisement, valuation.
s.m. A blackguard, lecher, libertine.
s.f. The twang (of a bow-string); sound; howling; surprise, wonder; fame, notoriety.
more: s. f. The twang (of a bowstring).

def: s.m. incorr. for tangār, q.v.
more: n. f. The twang (of a bowstring).

def: v.n. To sound; to strike (as a clock or gong); to shudder (?).
v. n. To strike.
s.m. An underground reservoir for water, a tank.
n. m. An underground reservoir for water, a tank. (?)
s. (in Guzerat) An under-ground reservoir for water, a tank.
s.f. Wages, pay, salary, allowance, an assignment (on the revenue), an order for wages, &c.
more: n. f. Monthly pay; allowance; salary, or wages ( talab ). tankhuāh baṭnā , v. n. Distribution of pay. tankhuāh-dār , n. m. One who receives a salary. tankhuāh denā , v. a. To pay (one's) salary, etc. tankhuāh-i-zātī , n. f. Personal allowance.
s. f. Wages, an assignment (on the revenues). تنخواه دار tanḵẖẉāh-dār, One who receives pay or salary, a holder of an order for wages. تنخواه داري tanḵẖẉāh-dār-ī, s. f. Receiving pay, service.
subs: s.m. One who receives pay or salary; a holder of an order for wages, &c.
s.f. Receiving pay or a salary; service
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تنخواه (tanxwāh).

def: s.f. A stone-cutter's chisel, a chisel; a tooth (as of a saw), a notch, jag; a dent, hollow, small hole, pit; a small piece cut or scooped out (as of a melon, &c. to examine the quality); a venereal chancre or shanker
more: s. f. 1. A venereal chancre or shanker. 2. A square piece cut out of a melon to examine its quality.
subs: (-kī), 'Builder's chisel to sound or to be busy,' a building or structure to be in course of erection.

def: n. f. S. टङ्क 1. A tooth; notch; jag. 2. A chisel. 3. A small hole; a hollow; pit. 4. A square piece cut out of a melon to examine its quality. Ṭāṅkī kī shart hai . Delhī Melon sellers' cry. 5. A venereal chancre or shanker. ṭāṅkī bajnā , v. n. lit . To go on with any work of masonry. To be built, erected, constructed.

ety: inf. n. ii of نقح 'to extract' (marrow), &c.
def: s.f. Cleaning, polishing; clearing (the bowels), purging; — deciding (disputes), investigation, examination, sifting, search, inquiry, ascertaining
more: n. f. نقح extracting the marrow. ( Med .) Clearing the bowels; physicking. tanqīh k . v. n. To fix; determine; lay down or draw up (the issues).
(inf. ii. of نقح) s. f. 1. Cleaning, polishing. 2. Deciding disputes, investigation, search, ascertaining.
subs: adj. Demanding inquiry or investigation; to be decided or determined; for decision
(-kī), To decide, determine; to ascertain; to sift, go to the bottom (of).

ety: ṭāṅk˚ = Prk. टक्क(इ) or टक्के(इ) = S. टङ्कय(ति) or टङ्क(ति), rt. टङ्क
def: v.t. To join, fasten, tie; to stitch; to cobble; to rivet; to solder; to cement; to tack (on or to), to append attach, annex; to make a note or memorandum of; to record, enter, pen; — to swallow, gulp down, eat up; — ṭāṅk-jānā, Idem
more: (trans. of ٿنکنا) v. a. To stitch, to cobble.
(see ٿنکار) v. a. To ring, to tinkle.

ety: ṭaṅk˚ = Prk. टंक(इ); S. टङ्क (ति), rt. टङ्क
def: v.n. To be stitched ( = ṭaknā, q.v.). v.t. To look at, view, gaze on, behold; to stare at; to watch for; — v.n. To peep, spy, watch; to aim (at, -par).

v. a. 1. To stitch; sew; cobble; pin. 2. To join; solder; cement; glue. 3. To join to; attach; annex; affix; append; tack to; fasten; file. 4. To write; pen; enter; book; record. 5. To petition ( tānnā , 4.); report against; complain. Ek arzī ṭāṅk do . 6. To eat; gulp down. Savā ser jalebyāṅ ṭāṅk gayā .
s.f. Twang, sound (of a bow)
v.n. To twang.

ety: for A. تنقية tanqiyat, inf. n. ii of نقي 'to extract marrow' (from)
def: s.m. Cleaning; clearing out (the bowels), purging; winnowing (grain); — deciding (cases); decision (cf. tanqīḥ).
more: (inf. ii. of نقي) s. m. 1. Purging, cleaning (the bowels). 2. Winnowing grain.

def: adj. & s.m. That alone, that merely, that itself.

ety: tān˚ = Prk. ताण(इ), or ताणे(इ) = S. तानय(ति), caus. of rt. तन्
def: v.t. To extend, stretch, expand; to pull, pull over (as a sheet, &c.); to tighten; to contract; knit (the brow); to pitch (a tent); to string up, to hang; to send in succession (as letters, &c.); to bring before (a magistrate), to sue; — v.n. To lie full length; to sleep; to die
more: v. a. S. तन to stretch out. See برَﮩـانا 2, 3. 1. To send; despatch. Do chiṭṭhiyāṅ tānī . 2. To commit; send to prison; hang. Chār pāṅch ko tān dīyā . 3. To tighten; tie; fasten; bind. 4. To complain; bring a charge against; sue. bhaveṅ tānnā , v. n. To knit the eyebrows. lambī tānnā , v. n. To sleep; to die ( marnā ). lambī chauṛī tānnā , v. n. To pull a long bow; to brag.
v. a. To extend, to stretch, to expand, to pull, to knit (the eyebrows). تنبوتاننا tambū tānnā, To pitch tents.

def: v.n. See تننا tannā.
more: v. n. S. तन् to stretch. 1. To stretch; spread; pull or be pulled tight. 2. To be pitched (as tents). 3. To sit upright or straight. 4. To look big; show off; to be a swell.
n. m. ( Slang ). Pudendum muliebre clitoris .
v. n. ( तनन ) 1. To stretch, to pull tight. 2. To sit upright.

def: v.t. See ٿاننا ṭānnā.
v.t. To pull, &c. ( = tānnā, the proper form).

v.n. = tannā, q.v. v.t. To extend, stretch, expand; to pull, pull over (as a sheet, &c.); to tighten; to contract; knit (the brow); to pitch (a tent); to string up, to hang; to send in succession (as letters, &c.); to bring before (a magistrate), to sue; — v.n. To lie full length; to sleep; to die

v.n. To stretch, be pulled tight; to be spun out; to spread; to be pitched (a tent); to sit upright or erect; to move or act in a pompous or conceited manner, to make a display; — v.t. To pull tight, &c. = tānnā, q.v.
v.n. 'To be stretched;' to be straight, to be erect; — to twang, sound (as the string of an instrument); to have a sharp pricking pain; to tingle ( = tantanānā).
more: v. n. 1. To be straight, erect. 2. To have an erection.
v. n. 1. To twang. 2. To have a sharp pricking pain from inflammation.

def: adj. & s.m. Facetious, jocose, jocund, ludicrous, playful, mirthful; — one who ridicules, a derider, mocker, scoffer.
more: adj. (superl. of طنز) Facetious, jocose, jocund, ludicrous, playful, mirthful.

ety: t̤annāz+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Playfulness, jocoseness, facetiousness; raillery, ridicule.
s.m. See s.v. tat. adj Heated, warmed; hot, warm; heating (cf. tattā).
Tir. n. m. A bat ( ballā ).

s.f. (prov.) = ṭāṅḍ, and ṭāṅḍī, qq.v. s.f. (m.?), A scaffolding (in a field of corn, &c. on which a person keeps watch when the grain is ripe to frighten away birds and other animals), a raised seat, stage, platform; a projecting sheif (in a wall).
s.m. Dancing (esp. with violent gesticulation); the frantic dance of the god Śiva and his votaries.

s.f. (prov.) = ṭāṅḍ, and ṭāṅḍī, qq.v. s.f. (m.?), A scaffolding (in a field of corn, &c. on which a person keeps watch when the grain is ripe to frighten away birds and other animals), a raised seat, stage, platform; a projecting sheif (in a wall).

def: s.f. Dividing in the middle, or into two equal parts; bisecting; bisection
more: n. f. نصف half. ( Geom .) Bisection.
(inf. ii. of نصف) s. f. Dividing in the middle or into two equal parts.

ety: inf. n. ii of نسخ 'to cancel,' &c.
def: s.f. Causing to annul or abrogate; cancelling; abrogation, quashing (an indictment or a decision).

ety: tāṅs˚ = S. त्रासय(ति), caus. or rt. त्रस्
def: v.t. To terrify, alarm, frighten; to threaten, menace; to treat with severity.
more: v. a. S. त्रस to frighten. 1. To menace; threaten; terrify. 2. To be unkind to or severe with; treat badly. Is sās ke barābar koī bahū ko nahīṅ tāṅstī .

def: s.f. Thread, fibre, &c.; catgut, sinew; wire; string of a musical instrument; — a loom
more: n. f. 1. The crown of the head. 2. ( Contemp .) Pate; noddle; skull. ṭāṅṭ khujānā , v. n. To feel itchy (the head). Chāṅṭe khāte khāte, ṭāṅṭ ganjī ho gaī . So many thumps he has had, he is left with no hair on the crown of his head. ṭāṅṭ ganjī honā , v. n. 1. To become bald from carrying loads, a beating, etc. 2. To be ruined (by lavish expenditure).

s. f. 1. ( तंत्री ) Catgut, sinew, the string of a musical instrument. تانت باجي اورراگ بوجها tānt bājī aur rāg būjhā, He understood from the first word. تانت باندهنا tānt bāndhnā, To put to silence an idle talker. 2. (from तन्तु ) A loom.
subs: lit. 'The string sounded and he knew the tune'; he understood from the first word

def: adj. & adv. Precise, exact; opportune; — precisely, exactly, just; opportunely; — s.m. Precise or exact moment, opportune moment, nick of time ( = tat samay), critical moment; need, occasion.
more: n. m. S. तत्व 1. See تت 1, 2. 2. The nick of time ( tant samā ); a critical moment; crisis. Rām nām sumran karo, yehī nām hai tant, Tīn lok, chaudhāṅ bhavan chhāë rahe Bhagvant . Dohā. Rām's name for aye dwell on, His name religion's essence is, Three worlds, seven regions up, seven down his Godhead all pervades. 3. Necessity; need; occasion.

def: s.f. Thread, string, wire, chord of a musical instrument ( = tāṅt, of which it may be a contraction)
s.m. Chief or essential part, essence, main point ( = tattwa); principal doctrine; model, typical form.
s.f. The crown of the head; (contemptuously) the pate, skull.
more: s. f. The crown of the head.

def: s.m. Series, row, range, line, string, train; race, posterity, generation.
more: n. m. S. तति Train; line; row. Gāṛiyoṅ kā tāṅtā lag rahā hai. naī tāṅtī , n. f. The present generation.

adj. Strong; sturdy; stout; hard. 1. Baṛā ṭāṅṭā javān hai . 2. Ajab saireṅ haiṅ dunyā meṅ, jo buḍḍhā hai woh tāṅṭā hai, Jo phal haigā dhatūrā hai, jo gul haigā woh kāṅṭā hai . Mahmūd. Strange sights this world displays, the old ones here are strong, The fruits are poisonous all, to roses thorns belong!
s. m. 1. A string (of camels, horses, &c.), a drove. 2. Row, range, series.

def: s.m. See ṭanṭā.
s.m. Wrangling, altercation, noisy squabble or quarrel, brawl, row
more: s. m. Wrangling, altercation, squabble, strife.
subs: adj. & s.m. Quarrelsome; — a quarrelsome person, a brawler.

def: n. m. Altercation; squabble; wrangling; brawl; row (jhagṛā) . Maiṅ ṭanṭe se kosoṅ bhāgtā hūṅ . Wom. ṭanṭe-bāz , Illit n. m. A litigious, quarrelsome person. ṭanṭe-bāz , Illit. adj. Litigious; quarrelsome ( jhagṛālū ).
more: n. m. 1. One who has no hand or arm. 2. A lazy fellow who wont work. ṭunḍiyāṅ baṅdhnā , v. n. To be handcuffed.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Well-knit, compact, stout, strong, powerful, sturdy; firm, solid; stiff, hard.
adj. = tanṭāl, q.v.
s.m. Quarrel, &c. = ṭanṭā, q.v.
subs: adj. & s.m. Quarrelsome; — a quarrelsome person ( = ṭanṭe-bāz).

s.m. Sound, noise, cry, shout, din, uproar; rumour, fame; pomp, dignity, state.
more: n. m. 1. A stalk or stem. 2. A button, knob; nipple. 3. The clitoris .

def: s.m. = tantanā, q.v. s.m. Sound, noise, cry, shout, din, uproar; rumour, fame; pomp, dignity, state.

ety: for A. طنطنة, rt. طنّ; see t̤anīn
def: s.m. Sound, din, clamour, hubbub, noise; fame; rumour; pomp, dignity, state; pride, vanity; imperiousness, domineering.
more: n. m. طن made a sound. 1. Show of authority; domineering; imperiousness. Ye tantanā apnī jurvā ko dikhāo, bandī nahīṅ uthāne kī! Mah. Wom. Go, show your rage to your wife, your humble servant (his mistress) wont endure it. 2. Childish passion; frivolous rage. Allah re tantanā! laṛkī to apne tehe meṅ āp jalī jātī hai . Mah. Wom. 3. Pride; vanity ( akaṛ , 7, 8). 4. Humours; airs. 1. Hākim hūṅ terī, hūṅ maiṅ Kalaṭṭar kī āshnā, Kyā tantanah dikhātī hai Tahsīldār kā? Rāhat. Your master I, for I'm the great Collector's friend, Cease then on Tahsīldār to plume yourself no end! 2. Chal, tere tantanah meṅ āg lagāūṅ! Mah. Wom.
s. m. 1. Sound, fame. 2. Rumour. 3. Pomp, dignity, state.

ety: fr. tantanānā; — ˚āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअ+ट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+त्वं
def: s.f. Tingling, smart; sharp pricking pain (arising from a burn or from inflammation).
more: (from تنتنانا) s. f. Sharp pricking pain from inflammation.

more: v. a. To twang; tingle. tantanā'haṭ , n. f. Sharp pricking pain from inflammation.
v. n. To twang, to tingle.

s. (dakh.) Adulteration, impostor ( Harris ).
adj. (f. -ī), Well-knit, compact, stout, strong, powerful, sturdy; firm, solid; stiff, hard.

ety: ṭāṅṭh ā+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: s.f. Compactness, closeness, stoutness, sturdiness, solidity, firmness, stiffness, hardness.
s. f. Solidity.
s.f. = tāntā, q.v. s.m. Series, row, range, line, string, train; race, posterity, generation.
s.m. A weaver.
s.m. A weaver.
more: n. f. A rude kind of sitār , q. v. 1. Apnī apnī tuntunī, aur apnā apnā rāg . Prov. Every one on his own pipe and each his own song. 2. Tuntunī bajāte mīyāṅ khāte shakkar, ghī, Naukrī kī aisī taisī, abke bache jī! Prov. I piped and thrummed a-tink-a-tink, was kept in sweets and buttered bread, This once with life escaped, I vow, to this villainous soldiering I ne'er will be led.

def: s. m. A musician.
adj. & s.m. Having wires or strings (as a musical instrument); — the music of a stringed instrument.

s.m. Leading or principal action of a ceremony, the regular order of ceremonies or rites, ritual; chief or essential part, main point; principal doctrine, rule, theory; a scientific work; a religious treatise teaching magical and mystic formularies for the worship of the deities or the attainment of superhuman power; a medicament, a drug, a chief remedy or charm considered as producing medicinal effects; charm, spell, incantation, enchantment; — demonstration, clear and right conclusion; the right way of doing anything; — raiment, vesture.
more: n. m. 1. Charm; enchantment; incantation. 2. A religious treatise, teaching peculiar and mystical forms and rites for the worship of the deities or attainment of superhuman power.

s. m. 1. Name of a religious treatise teaching peculiar and mystical forms and rites for the worship of the deities or the attainment of superhuman power. It is mostly in the shape of a dialogue between Siva and Durgā, who are the peculiar deities of the Tantrikas. 2. Charm, enchantment.

def: s.m. A musician; — one who follows a tantra; a practiser of enchantments.
adj. & s.m. Relating to the Tāntras; taught or contained in the Tāntras; following the Tāntras; — one who is completely versed in any science or system; a scholar; a philosopher.

n. f. ṭankā , n. m. 1. Tinkle; the sound emitted by China. 2. Throbbing (of the temples). 3. Spirit; mettle.

adj. or adv. Slight, small, a little. adv. Slightly.

s.f. See s.v. tan.
s.f. Keeping apart from all impurity; purifying, cleansing; purity, holiness.
more: (inf. ii. of نزه) s. f. Keeping apart from all impurity; purity, holiness.

ety: inf. n. ii of نزل 'to descend'
def: s.f. Causing (a traveller, &c.) to alight, receiving (one) hospitably; revelation; the Qorʼān.
more: (inf. ii. of نزل) s. f. 1. Revelation. 2. The ḳurʼān. 3. Causing (a traveller) to alight, receiving him hospitably.

ety: inf. n. ii of نظم 'to join, string,' &c.
def: Composing (verses); stringing or threading (pearls, &c.); ordering, arranging.
more: (inf. ii. of نظم) s. f. 1. Ordering, arranging. 2. Composing verses. 3. Threading pearls.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تنظیم (tanzīm), from Arabic تَنْظِيم (tanẓīm).

ety: inf. n. ii of نظر 'to look' (at)
def: s.f. Beholding, contemplating; — likening, resembling.
more: (inf. ii. of نظر) s. f. 1. Likening, resembling. 2. Beholding, contemplating.

ety: for A. تنزية tanziyat, inf. n. ii of نزو 'to overpower'
def: s.m. Inspiring with desire; desire; propensity.
more: (inf. ii. of نزو or نزا) s. m. Inspiring with desire; desire, propensity.

def: s.m. Heating (metals, &c.); heat, fusion; proof, trial, assay; passion, wrath, rage; strength, power; speed; splendour, dignity (these five meanings are peculiar to the Persian tāv, which, as regards the first three, is related to the Sanskrit tavās); — turning or twisting (metals, orig. by heating); contortion, twist, coil, curl, roll, fold; a sheet of paper
more: n. m. P. تاب S. ताप to heat. 1. The act of heating (metal); heat; fusion. Ek tāo kī kasar hai . 2. Passion; rage; anger; wrath. 3. Twist; coil; contortion. 4. A sheet of paper. tāo bhāo , adj. Very little. tāo denā , v. n. 1. To heat; melt; fuse. 2. To twist (string, moustache, etc.). tāo khānā , v. n. 1. To be burnt, overdone. 2. To be overheated (the blood). 3. To be inflamed, enraged, excited.

s. m. 1. Heat. 2. Passion, rage. 3. Strength, power. 4. Splendour, dignity. 5. Twist, coil, contortion. تاودينا tāʼo denā, 1. To twist. 2. To stroke the whiskers. 3. To heat (as, iron). تاوکهانا tāʼo khānā, 1. To be heated. 2. To be angry.
s. m. 1. A sheet of paper. 2. Proof, trial, essay. 3. Speed.
subs: s.m. Name of a powder or substance which, mixed with gold or silver, prevents the detection of adulteration
(-ko), To heat; to anneal; to melt, fuze; — to twist (cord, &c.), to curl or twist (the mustache, &c.)
v.n. To be heated, be made red hot; to be overdone, be scorched or burnt; to be overheated (as the blood); to be inflamed, be enraged.

def: adv. Rapidly, fast, quick.
more: adv. Fast; quick; rapidly. Nāk nāthe, kamar bande, raṅg wākā sāṅolā, Chāl chalte gābh de, chāl chale woh tāolā . Riddle. Ans. Charkhā . A spinning wheel. Her nose is bored, her waist is girt, her color is nut brown, As fast she moves all from her pregnant womb her issue's thrown. tāo'lā, tāvalyā , n. m. See utāvlā . tattā tāolā , adj. Hasty; precipitate.

ety: tāʼo˚ = Prk. ताव(इ), or तावे(इ) = S. तापय(ति), caus. of rt. तप्
def: v.t. = tānā, q.v. s.m. The threads that are extended lengthwise upon a loom, the warp
more: v. a. 1. To heat, to raise heat by blowing with bellows, to anneal. 2. To prove, to assay. 3. To twist.
subs: v.n. To fidget, to dance attendance
(-ke), To set or dispose the warp (of a piece of cloth); to weave; — to wander hither and thither without profit; to plan and exert oneself to no purpose.

def: s.m. Warmth, heat, burning; fever (com. tap); affliction, pain, sorrow, distress; passion
more: Rus. n. m. S. तप burn. 1. Warmth; heat. 2. Fever. Jo bar dekhe mujhe tāvan āve, soī bar mujhe biyāhan āve . Prov. Oh such a bridegroom's come to marry me! it puts me in a fever him only to see. tāp-tillī, tāb-tillī , n. f. An induration of the spleen attended with, or preceded by fever; splenitis .

n. f. 1. The hoof of a horse. 2. A tap; whack; hit; a sound of beating. 3. Pawing; the tramp of a horse's hoofs. Miyāṅ āyā kyoṅ-kar jānye, Ghoṛe kī ṭāp se paihchāṅye . Nursery rhyme. When young master comes home how are we to know, Click click his horse's hoofs will clattering go. 4. The penis , generally ṭappū or ṭāp-dār .
1. s. m. Warmth, heat. 2. s. f. Fever. تاپ تلي tāp-tillī, s. f. See تاب تلي under تاب tāb.
subs: s.f. Induration or enlargement of the spleen attended with or preceded by fever; splenitis, ague-cake ( = tāb-tillī)
s.m. Remover of sorrow or distress, an epithet of a deity.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit ताप (tāpá), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *tāpás, from Proto-Indo-European *tep-.

ety: Pehl. tap; Zend tafnu, rt. tap; S. तप, or ताप
def: s.f. Heat, warmth; fever; — tap-utarnā, v.n. Fever to go off
more: n. m. S. तप heat. Fever. tap utarnā , v. n. To go off (a fever). tap chaṛhnā , v. n. To come on (a fever).
n. m. 1. A cover; covering; hood. 2. The fly of a tent. 3. Spring; leap; bound.
1. ( तप ) s. f. Heat, warmth. 2. ( तपः ) s. m. Devout austerity, religious penance.

s. f. A fever. تپ اترنا tap utarnā, To go off (a fever). تپ چڙهنا tap chaṛhnā, To come on (a fever). تپ دق tap-i-diḳḳ, s. f. A hectick fever. تپ غب tap-i-g̠ẖibb, s. f. A tertian fever. تپ کا موت جانا tap kā mūt jānā, The breaking out of an eruption on the lips after a fever. تپ لرزه tap-i-larza, s. m. An ague. تپ محرق tap-i-muḥriḳ, s. m. An ardent or burning fever. تپ مواظبه tap-i-muwāz̤aba, A quotidian fever. تپ نوبت tap-i-naubat, s. f. An intermittent fever.
subs: s.f. Induration and enlargement of the spleen, &c. = tāp-tiliī, q.v.s.v. tāp
s.m. Breaking out on the lips after a fever, or of a fever in the system; the thrush
s.m. The breaking out of an eruption on the lips after a fever

def: s.m. Devout austerity, religious penance, &c. ( = tapas, q.v.); the hot season, the height of summer
subs: adj. Depending on the merit of penance
s.m. One who depends for salvation on the merit of his deeds of penance

def: v. n. To jump over.
v. a. To thrust oneʼs self into other peopleʼs concerns, to intermeddle, to interfere.
Ague ( Harris ).

s.f. Hoof (of a horse); stroke (of a horse's forefoot), pawing; sound (of a horse's hoof, or of beating, &c.), tramp (of a horse); tap, whack; patting, stroking, feeling (cf. thāp); — a bamboo frame for catching fish ( = ṭāpā)
more: s. f. 1. A stroke with the fore foot of a horse. 2. The sound of a horseʼs hoof in travelling. 3. A frame made of bamboo for catching fish.
subs: (or ṭāp-ā-ṭoʼiyā) karnā, v.n. To feel about (for), to grope, to rummage; to trifle; to find out and repair (leaks in a thatched roof)
v.n. To paw or beat the ground (a horse), &c. = ṭāpnā, q.v.

def: s.m. Covering, cover, the upper part or fly (of a tent); the hood (of a carriage, &c.); sound of dropping (as of rain, &c.); dropping, patter, drop (of rain, &c.); — spring, leap, bound
more: s. m. Spring, leap, bound, a drop of rain or other liquid. 2. A sound.
subs: ṭap-ṭap, adv. With a sound like that of a dropping liquid; drop-drop, patter-patter; in continuous drops, in a shower (as rain, tears, bullets, &c.); one after another, continuously, successively.

def: s.m. The hot season, a season of great heat ( = tap, q.v.); — a devotee, ascetic, &c. ( = tapasī, tapasvī, q.v.).
more: n. m. The hottest days in summer.
s. m. A worshipper, an ascetick, a devotee.

def: s.m. = tappā; ṭappā, q.v.

s.m. A bell-shaped covering of bamboo-work for placing over fowls; a hen-coop; a bamboo frame for catching fish ( = ṭāp); a framework covered with net or muslin placed over a cradle; a framework cover generally (with or without any external covering; and, locally, anything with such a cover, as a hut, a boat or raft, &c.; also a covering of cloth, &c.; cf. ṭap).
more: s. m. A hen-coop. ٿاپا ٿوٴي or ٿاپا ٿوٴيا کرنا ṭāpā-ṭoʼī or ṭāpā-ṭoʼiyā-karnā, 1. To trifle. 2. To grope, to rummage.

def: s.m. (local), An island in a river (cf. ṭāpū).

n. m. H. ṭapnā to cover. 1. A hen-coop ( khāṅchā ). 'Bol mere murge 'kukṛūṅ kū.' 'Jal gayā ṭāpā, rah gayā tū.' Double entendre . Boys' rhyme. Bravo young Cock, cry 'Cock-a-doodle-doo!' 'Burnt up your hen-coop's now, left only you!' 2. ( Auctioneers ) A crinoline. 3. A bamboo frame for catching fish. ṭāpā ṭoī k . v. a. 1. To grope for; rummage. 2. E. To repair the leaks (in a thatched roof).
s.m. = tapāk (the more com. form, q.v.).
more: s. m. Friendship, the anguish of love, solicitude of friendship, affection.

def: adj. & s.m. Heating, burning, inflaming; — fever, morbid heat.
s.f. Sound of dropping (of a liquid, &c.); dropping, dripping; drop; beat, pulsation, throbbing, throb, pain
more: (from ٿپکنا) s. f. 1. Pain, throbbing. 2. Sound made by dropping. ٿپک پڙنا ṭapak paṛnā, To drip. ٿپک نويس ṭapak-navīs, s. m. One who reports erroneously what he pretends to have overheard.
subs: s.m. One who reports erroneously what he pretends to have overheard.

def: s.m. Warmth, ardour, fervour, zeal; the anguish of love; solicitude of friendship; love, affection, friendship; apparent cordiality; — affliction, distress, uneasiness, disquietude; consternation; palpitation ( = tapak).
more: (Pers. تاپاک) s. m. 1. Affection, esteem, regard, love, zeal, ardour. 2. Consternation, affliction, uneasiness.

def: s.f. Throb, palpitation (cf. tapāk).
more: n. f. S. तप heat. Throb; palpitation.
n. f. 1. Drippings; dribbling; drops; percolation. 2. E. Throbbing; pain. ṭapak paṛnā , v. n. To drip; drop.

def: s.m. An officer formerly employed in revenue affairs.
more: n. m. 1. A musketeer; rifleman; gunner. 2. A revenue officer (formerly).

ety: tapak˚ = S. तप+कृ; or = thapak˚, q.v.
def: v.n. To throb, to palpitate.
more: v. n. 1. To drip; dribble; distil; trickle; ooze; drizzle; leak. 1. Pakke ām ke ṭapakne kā ḍar hai . Prov. When mangoes ripe have grown, their juices then are apt to run. (Full of years, death appears). 2. Ek ek roṭī, do do boṭī, survā ṭapaktā jāë, Are e logo, gilā na kījo, mere ghar kāj hai! Song. One whole cake each, two bits of meat, with gravy thin as may be, You cant complain, my gentle friends, to-day a feast I've made ye! 3. Ṭapkī paṛtī hai us se gadrāhaṭ, Hai ye ālam terī javānī kā . Wom. Raṅgīṅ. Her ripening bloom with juices bursting full, Hers is the wanton spring of days youthful. 4. Ām pake, mahuā gadrāne, ṭapak-ṭapak ras jāë, Pī bin mo pe rahiyo na jāë . Wom. The mangoes ripening are, the mauvas' juices burst and drop, My husband's absence tries me sore, I can no longer stop. 5. Nanadī chhavāve apnā baṅgalvā, Hamaro chhavaiyā pardesvā, Bundiyā ṭapake rī! Mag. song. My husba…
v. n. To throb, to palpitate. تپکنا پهوڙي کا tapaknā phoṛe kā, The throbbing of a phlegmon or bile, when coming to suppuration.

def: v.n. To drop (as a liquid, or as ripe fruit); to drip, dribble, distil, exude, leak; to beat, throb, palpitate; to ache, pain (cf. tapaknā)
more: v. n. 1. To drop when ripe. 2. To drip, to distil. 3. To throb, to palpitate.
subs: v.n. To drip; to drop off or down (as ripe fruit, &c.).

def: s.m. Heating, warming, burning, inflaming; distressing; the sun; name of a division of hell.

ety: imperf. part. of tapīdan, 'to tremble,' &c.; rt. Zend. tap = S. तप्
def: adj. Growing hot; becoming agitated; palpitating; trembling.
more: part. act. (of تپيدن) Palpitating, growing hot.

def: s.f. Heat, warmth, burning, glow, fervour; — s.m. The sun; the hot season; name of one of the divisions of hell; mental distress, grieving, pining.
more: n. f. S. टिप्पनी See پتري Annotation.
s. f. 1. Heat, glow, fervour. 2. The sun.

def: part. adj. = تپان tapān, q.v. s.m. Heating, warming, burning, inflaming; distressing; the sun; name of a division of hell.
more: part. act. (of طپيدن) Palpitating, flouncing, agitated.

ety: tapā˚ = Prk. तवावे(इ) = S. तप+आपय˚ caus. augment
def: v.t. To heat, to warm; to cause to glow; to test (metals) by means of fire; to make (a person) warm himself (at the fire or in the sunshine).
more: v. a. S. तप To heat (solids); to test (gold or silver) in the fire. Kharā, khoṭā tapāne se mālūm hotā hai . Prov. Saying. Good and bad the fire (or adversity) doth prove.
v. a. To pour a libation.
( तापन or caus. of تپنا) v. a. To heat, warm, make glow, make another person warm himself at the fire or in the sun.

ety: tap˚ = S. तर्पय˚ (caus. of rt. तृप्)+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.t. To present a libation to (the gods or to the manes); to present an offering.
v.t. To cause to jump or leap over; to cause to bound, &c.
more: (caus. of ٿپنا) v. a. To cause to jump over, to cause to leap, to make to bound.

def: s.f. A name of the Godavery river, or of the Tāpī.
adj. & s.m. Performing penance, practising austerities; devout; — a practiser of religious austerities, a devotee, ascetic, hermit.

ety: S. तपस्; or S. ताप+ās = S. वाञ्छा
def: s.f. Sunshine, the sunbeams; labour, toil.
more: s. f. 1. Sunshine, the sunbeams. 2. Toil, labour.

def: s.m. Heat; fire (esp. as an instrument of self-torture); pain, suffering, torture; religious austerity, penance mortification, the practice of mental or personal self-denial or the infliction of bodily torture; the meditation connected with such a practice; — moral virtue, merit; special observance or duty of any particular caste (e.g. the tapas of a Brāhman is sacred learning; of a Kshatriya, the protection of subjects; of a Vaiśya, giving alms to Brāhmans; of a Śūdra, service; and of a Rishi or saint, feeding upon herbs and roots); one of the seven worlds, inhabited by saints or devotees after death; — the hot season
more: n. f. 1. Claim; ground; footing; standing. 2. Combination; alliance; coalition; confederacy. ṭippas jamānā, yā lagānā , v. n. 1. To make or lay a foundation. 2. To make interest; secure one's good offices. 3. See panīrī jamānā .
s. (dakh.) Religious austerity, mortification of self, penance.
subs: s.m. The power acquired by religious austerities
adj. & s.m. Rich in religious penance; ascetic, pious; — one who treasures up the merit of mortifications and austerities, an ascetic, a devotee, one who performs religious penance
s.m. One of the seven worlds (see above)
s.m. 'Enclosure or district of religious penance,' a name applied to the holy land situated in central India
tapo-ban, s.m. 'Penance grove,' a sacred grove in which ascetics perform their religious austerities

def: s.f. corr. of tapasyā, q.v. s.f. Devout austerity, religious penance.
s. f. A female devotee.
adj. & s.m. = tapasvī, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Engaged in the practice of rigorous and devout penance; ascetic, devout; — an ascetic, devotee, a jogī.
more: s.m. A devotee, a performer of austere devotion, an ascetick. 2. adj. Devout, practising austere devotion. تپسي مچهلي tapassī machhlī, s. f. The mango-fish. (Polynemus paradisea).

def: adj. & s.m. = tapasya, q.v.
subs: tapsī-maććhī, tapsī-maćh, s.f. The mango fish, Polynemus risua or Paradiseus.

def: = H تپشيا तपश्या tapaśyā, see tapassī, and tapasyā.
more: n. m. An ascetic; a devotee.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Engaged in the practice of rigorous and devout penance; ascetic, devout; — an ascetic, devotee, a jogī.
ex: یہ تپسوی جنگل میں رہتے ہیں۔
ye tapasvī jaṅgal mẽ rahte ha͠i.
These ascetics live in the forest.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit तपस्वी (tapasvī).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Engaged in the practice of rigorous and devout penance; ascetic, devout; — an ascetic, devotee, a jogī.
s.f. Devout austerity, religious penance.
more: s. f. Devout austerity, religious penance, devotion.

def: adj. & s.m. Produced by heat; — the month Phāgun (Feb.-March); — religious penance, &c. ( = tapasyā).
= H تپشي तपश्शी tapaśśī, see tapassī, and tapasyā.
more: n. f. S. तप to heat. Religious penance. See H. تپ Bhāgīrath ne karī tapashshyā Har charnan se lagāyā man, Tab āīṅ brammāṅḍal se srī Gaṅgā jī tāran taran . Then Bhagīrat sacrificed, his mind on Harī's feet deep set, When from the mundane egg were Ganges' saving waters let.

def: adv. Without ceasing (the sound of falling drops, or the pattering of rain); one after another; successively. 1. Gup chup koṭhṛī meṅ baiṭhe haiṅ, āṅsū ṭap ṭap girte haiṅ . Alone her silent room she keeps, the tears keep coursing down her cheeks. 2. Ṭapā-ṭap lagīṅ golyāṅ bājne, Tillaṅgoṅ kī ṭopī lagī nāchne . Fast flew the bullets after one another, The soldiers' caps went dancing hither thither.

r: ṭapā-ṭoʼā, ṭappā-ṭoʼā, ṭapā-ṭoʼī, ṭappā-ṭoʼī, ṭapnā, ṭo-nā, ṭāpā-ṭoʼī, ṭāp
def: s.m. = H ٿپاٿوئي टपाटोई ṭapā-ṭoʼī, टप्पाटोईṭappā-ṭoʼī, s.f. (from ṭapnā and ṭo-nā), Feeling (for), groping, passing the hand over ( = ṭāpā-ṭoʼī, q.v.s.v. ṭāp).

r: ṭapā-ṭoʼī, ṭappā-ṭoʼī, ṭapā-ṭoʼā, ṭappā-ṭoʼā, ṭapnā, ṭo-nā, ṭāpā-ṭoʼī, ṭāp
def: s.f. = H ٿپاٿوا टपाटोआ ṭapā-ṭoʼā, टप्पाटोआṭappā-ṭoʼā, s.m. (from ṭapnā and ṭo-nā), Feeling (for), groping, passing the hand over ( = ṭāpā-ṭoʼī, q.v.s.v. ṭāp).
s.m. Pouring out a libation; a libation.
v.t. = tapānā, q.v. v.t. To heat, to warm; to cause to glow; to test (metals) by means of fire; to make (a person) warm himself (at the fire or in the sunshine).
adj. & s.m. Heated, glowing; oppressed by heat; suffering from disease (moral or physical); — anything heated or glowing.
more: s. f. Name of a river.

def: s.f. Name of the Surāt river (also called Tāptī); — a name of the Jamnā river.
adj. & s.m. = tapasvī, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Engaged in the practice of rigorous and devout penance; ascetic, devout; — an ascetic, devotee, a jogī.
more: s. m. A devotee, a worshipper. See تپا tapā, and تپسي tapsī.

def: part. (of t̤apīdan), Agitated, distressed (esp. with heat), heated; palpitating, fluttering.
more: part. pass. (of طپيدن) Agitated, palpitating, flouncing, fluttering.

ety: rt. Zend tap+tan; S. तप्+तं; with conjunct. ī
def: adj. & adv. Agitated, troubled; — in a troubled condition, in a state of agitation.
more: s. m. Palpitating, flouncing, agitation, tremour. تپيدن وار tapīdan-wār, adj. Agitated, troubled.

def: s.m. = tapīdan, q.v. adj. & adv. Agitated, troubled; — in a troubled condition, in a state of agitation.
n. f. A hectic or chronic fever; phthisis; pulmonary consumption. tap kā mūt jānā , v. n. To break out (an eruption on the lips after a fever).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَپِ دِق (tap-i diq). By surface analysis, تپ + دق.

def: s.f. Heat, warmth; distress (esp. that caused by heat); affliction; agitation; palpitation.
more: (from تپيدن) s. f. 1. Affliction, agitation, palpitation. 2. Heat, warmth.

def: s.f. = tapish, q.v. s.f. Heat, warmth; distress (esp. that caused by heat); affliction; agitation; palpitation.
part. Heated, inflamed; distressed, pained.
s.m. Continuous dropping, dripping; a drop (of rain, &c., cf. ṭipkā); fruit falling when ripe (particularly mangoes), a windfall
more: s. m. 1. A drop of rain. 2. Fruit falling when ripe (particularly mangos), a windfall.
subs: s.f. Continuous dropping, dripping, trickle; a continuous dropping or falling off (as of fruit, or customers, or of men or animals under epidemic or murrain)
(-kā), To drip; to become leaky, to leak.

def: n. m. 1. Dropping; dripping; trickling; leak. 2. Falling of ripened fruit (particularly mangoes); a windfall. 3. Sprinkling any color with the fingers. ṭapkā-ṭapkī , n. f. 1. Dropping; trickling; drizzle. 2. Dropping (of fruit, customers, etc.) one after another. 3. Dying one after another. Ādmiyoṅ kī ṭapkā ṭapkī lag rahī hai . Men are dropping off one after the other. ṭapkā lagnā , v. n. To leak; become leaky. Āmoṅ kā ṭapkā lag rahā hai .
v. a. See تَپکنا
v.t. To cause to drop or drip, to distil.
more: (causal of ٿپکنا) v. a. To cause to drip, to distil.

ety: rt. of ṭapkānā+āw = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं
def: s.m. Dripping; distillation.
n. m. Dripping; distillation.
s.m. See s.v. tap. s.m. Warmth, heat, burning; fever (com. tap); affliction, pain, sorrow, distress; passion
more: s. m. Breaking out on the lips after a fever, the thrush.
subs: s.f. Induration or enlargement of the spleen attended with or preceded by fever; splenitis, ague-cake ( = tāb-tillī)
s.m. Remover of sorrow or distress, an epithet of a deity.

ety: ṭapak, q.v.+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. Friskiness, skittishness
more: s. f. diminutive of ٿپکا ṭapkā, q. v. ٿپکي پڙني عورت کي ṭapkī paṛnī aﻌurat kī, To be wanton (a woman).
subs: (-kī), To become frisky or skittish, to become wanton (a woman).

ety: tāp˚ = S. तापय(ति), caus. of rt. तप्;+nā = anā = Prk. अणअं = S.अनीयं
def: v.t. To heat, warm, warm oneself (at or over a fire, e.g. āg tāpnā); — v.n. To bask (in the sun or before a fire).
more: v. n. To warm one's self at (or over) a fire or in the sun. 1. Jidhar jaltā dekheṅ, tidhar hī ko tāpeṅ . Prov. Where'er a fire she haps to see to warm herself she squats quite free. 2. Phūṅkne ke na phāṅkne ke, ṭāṅg uṭhā-ke tāpne ke . Prov. She 'll neither blow nor stir the fire, but she 'll up with her legs to warm o'er the fire. 3. Āg tāpeṅ kahāṅ talak insāṅ, Dhūp khāveṅ kahāṅ talak jāṅdār? Gālib. (The poet humorously expostulates with the king of Dehli in verse regarding his arrears of Pension). How long before the fire my naked body warm, Or wanting coals sit in the sun to keep just warm? 4. Kisī kā ghar jale, aur koī tāpe . Prov. One man's house burns, another warms himself by the fire.

v. n. 1. To paw (a horse). 1. Na khāve, na pīve, na sove kabhī, Na ṭāpe, na bīmār hove kabhī . The winged horse. Mīr Hasan. Nor eats, nor drinks, nor ever sleeps, Nor paws, nor ails, his course he keeps. 2. To stamp with impatience; to be imtient. 3. To cool one's heels; to be kept waiting. 4. To be kicking about; to saunter idly; to find oneself too late. Āj akele hī ṭāp rahe ho . 5. To stand gaping; to be petrified; to be lost in vain regrets. Yār chalā gayā, maiṅ ṭāptā rah gayā . 6. To spring; scale; leap over. Āj kal to divāleṅ ṭāpte ho! Now-a-days you are given to scaling walls. (He slips into womens' apartments). 7. To look about or search in vain. Woh naukrī ke vāste mahīnoṅ se ṭāp rahā hai .
v. a. To bask in the sun or before a fire, to warm oneʼs self at (or over) a fire, to warm, to heat.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀢𑀧𑁆𑀧𑀇 (tappaï), from Sanskrit तप्य॑ति (tápyati). Cognate with Punjabi ਤਪਣਾ (tapṇā) / تَپݨا (tapṇā), Sindhi تَپَڻُ (tapaṇu) / तपणु, Gujarati તાપવું (tāpvũ), Marathi तापणे (tāpṇe), Sinhalese තපිනවා (tapinawā).

ety: tap˚ = Prk. तप्प(इ) = S. तप(ति) or तप्य(ति), rt. तप्
def: v.n. To be heated; to glow; to burn with pain or grief; to frisk about; to shine, to be glorious.
more: v. n. 1. To be heated; to glow. Jitnā tapegā utnā barsegā . Prov. As the heat so will be the Rains. 2. To shine; to be glorious; to be held in awe. Rāj taptā hai . 3. To burn with pain or grief.
v. n. See الانگنا 1, 3. 1. To frisk; gambol. 2. To cover; superpose. 3. To top; mount; ride; cross; copulate.
v. a. 1. To jump across; clear. 2. To top; cover; pair.
v. n. 1. To be glorified. 2. To glow, to be heated, to frisk about.

def: v.n. To cover, to superpose; to put the hand (upon), pass the hand (over); to top, mount; to copulate, couple, pair (as birds, &c.); to jump (over or across), to vault; to jump about, be skittish, to frisk, gambol
more: v. a. To jump over. ٿپا ٿوا ṭapā ṭoʼā, part. Groping.
subs: (-ko), To spring or vault (over), to leap or jump (over or across); to jump down (-meṅ, upon, or in the midst of); (fig.) to thrust oneself (into the concerns of another), to intermeddle, interfere

def: v.n. To paw or beat the ground (a horse); to beat the feet on the ground, to stamp (in impatience, or pain, or despair, &c.), to be impatient, restless, or agitated; to tramp about; — v.t. To leap or spring over (a wall, &c.), to scale ( = ṭapnā).
more: v. n. 1. To paw with the fore feet (as, a horse that is impatient). 2. To whore. (Low phrase).

def: s.m. See s.v. tapas for compounds of which tapo is the first member. adj. & s.m. Performing penance, practising austerities; devout; — a practiser of religious austerities, a devotee, ascetic, hermit.

s.m. Spring, leap, jump (in measure); bound, range (of a ball, bullet, &c.); distance, intervening space, interval (cf. pallā); a long stitch (in sewing); a relay, post (of ḍāk-bearers, post-runners, &c.); station for relays, post-station, stage, halting or resting-place; a post office; a raft, a catamaran (cf. ṭāpā); a mode (in music); a kind of hook; a small tract or division of country (smaller than a pargana, and consisting, generally, of a large town with villages and lands dependent; — ṭappā ḍālnā (-meṅ), To put in long stitches, to use long stitches, to baste
more: s. m. 1. The postoffice. 2. The name of a mode in musick. 3. The bound of a ball, &c. 4. A sort of hook. 5. A jump, a bound. ٿپا مارنا ṭappā-mārnā, 1. To sew or stitch with intervals. 2. To read in a desultory manner. ٿپا کهانا ṭappā khānā, To bound or ricochet (a shot).
subs: v.n. To bound or ricochet (a ball)
v.n. To bound; to make a long jump, take a long leap; to skip (in reading), to read in a desultory way; to baste ( = ṭappā ḍālnā).

def: s.m. = ٿپا ṭappā, q.v.
s.m. properly ṭappā, q.v.
more: n. m. 1. Bound; leap; spring (a ball, etc.). 2. Distance; reach; range; shot. Do golī ke ṭappe par jā paṛā . 3. A relay; a ḍāk . 4. A mode in music. 5. A raft; a catamaran. 6. A kind of hook. (?) 7. E. A small tract or division of country smaller than a Parganā , comprising one or more villages. ṭappā khānā , v. n. To bound or ricochet. ṭappā mārnā , v. n. 1. To baste (E. laṅgar denā ). 2. To read in a desultory way.

def: s.m. (dialec.) A post-station; post-office; post, mail; a relay (of runners, &c.)
more: s. (dakh.) A packet or mail (of letters, Harris ); the post.

s. m. (dakh.) Post-man.
n. f. Searching about; groping.
s.m. A thatched house, a hut, &c. (see ṭāpā).
n. m. A thatched house; a hut.

def: s.f. A mound; a small eminence or hillock, an acclivity; a limitary dike ( = meṅṛ).
more: s. f. A mound, a small height, an acclivity.

s.f. Heat, &c. (see tapish).
s.m. An island; a shoal ( = ćar).

s.m. A mineral substance, sulphuret of iron (said to be obtained from the river Tāpī).

ety: S. तन्त्र; and cf. S. तारः
def: s.m. Thread, string; the warp or threads extended lengthwise in a loom; wire, cord, string of a musical instrument; uninterrupted series, line, continuation, succession; a letter-file; the electric telegraph ( = ṭār-barqī); a telegraphic message, telegram; a hair of a curl or ringlet; the hair-worm or guinea worm; the snout, sucking tube, or trunk of most winged insects; the antennæ or feelers of prawns, flies, &c.; a bit, piece
more: n. m. S. तन to stretch. 1. A thread; string. 2. The warp. 3. A wire; a string of a musical instrument. 4. A letter file. 5. The electric telegraph ( tār-barqī ). 6. A telegram. Tār āyā hai . 7. The guinea worm ( nārū ). 8. The snout, sucking tube, or trunk of most winged insects; the horns, antennæ or feelers of prawns. 9. One hair of a curl or ringlet. 10. Continuation; succession; line; series. Kuchh bolī ṭholī piyār-bharī, kuchh gālī hai, kuchh mālī hai; In charchoṅ kā, in chohloṅ kā ye tār lagāyā Holī ne . Nazīr. Here honeyed words of amorous pairs, there wanton chaff and themes, Such sweet discourse, such prurient jokes doth Holi pour in streams. tār baṅdhnā , v. n. To be continued or repeated without interruption. Kyā hī chaman meṅ shab ko wāh barse thi nūr kī jhaṛī, Tār nashoṅ ke the baṅdhe, loṭe thī chāṅdnī paṛī . Nazīr. How silvery bright with moon beams showered the flower-deck…

n. m. S. ताल् A palm tree; the Palmyra or Borassus flabelliformis , and the Coryphatolliera . The name is most appropriate to the Palmyra, from the stem of which is extracted the juice which becomes tāṛī , or vulgarly, toddy. The corypha does not yield the same, but its leaves are used for writing upon. tāṛ-ban . A plantation of palm trees; a grove or 'tope' of palmyra trees. tāṛ-pattar . The palm-leaf prepared for writing, or a document so written.

s. m. f. 1. A thread. 2. A wire, the string of a musical instrument, a chord. 3. Darkness, obscurity. 4. The warp or threads placed lengthwise in weaving a piece of cloth. تارباندهنا tār bāndhnā, To continue or repeat an action without interruption. تارٿوٿنا tār ṭūṭnā, To be disjointed, to be separated. تارعنکبوت tār-i-aﻌnkabūt, Cobweb. تارکش tār-kash, s. m. A wiredrawer. تارکشي tār-kash-ī, s. f. 1. A kind of needle work. 2. Wiredrawing. تارکشي کرنا tār-kash-ī karnā, To draw wires, to wiredraw.

def: s.m. The palmyra-tree or fan-palm, Borassus flabelliformis; the tree Corypha taliera (the leaves of which are used for writing upon)
more: s. m. The palm tree (Borassus flabelliformis).
(from تاڙنا) s.f. Understanding. تاڙباز tāṛbāz, adj. Intelligent, quick of apprehension. تاڙبازي tāṛ-bāz-ī, s. f. Quickness of apprehension.
s. m. The palmyra tree or fan palm.

s.m. The gelatinous pulp of the fruit of the palmyra tree.

ety: Zend tauruna; S. taruṇ, rt. tr̤ī
def: adj. New, fresh; green; young, tender, soft; juicy, moist, damp, wet, wet through, saturated (with moisture, or grease, &c.); refreshed, revived, gladdened; — loose, large (as shoes, &c.)
more: adj. 1. Moist; wet; saturated. 2. Fresh; juicy; ripe. 3. Greasy; rich. Tarmāl jalebī! Street cry. 4. Refreshed; revived; gladdened. 5. Large; loose. Yeh jūtī zarā tar hai . 6. The sign of the Comparative degree. tar-ba-tar ; E. tartarātā , adj. 1. Completely wet; dripping; saturated. 2. Very greasy; very rich. tar zamīn , n. f. Moist land. tar k , v. a. To wet; steep ( bhigonā ). tar o tāzah , adj. Fresh and ripe; quite fresh.
n. m. S. तड An imitative sound like pat, patter. taṛā-taṛ , n. m. A pattering sound; successive blows.

n. m. 1. The croaking of a frog. 2. Roughness; violence; pugnacity. Shekhoṅ kī shekhī, Paṭhānoṅ kī ṭar . Prov. The pride of a Sheikh (priest), the insolence of a Paṭhān (soldier) 3. E. A crane. 4. The day after Id . q. v.; the day after the fair. Īd pīchhe ṭar, barāt pīchhe dhauṅsā . Prov. After the festival, rejoicing; after the wedding, music!

adj. 1. Moist, damp, wet, fresh, green, young, juicy. تربندي tar-baṇd-ī, s. f. The application of a wet bandage to a wound. 2. A particle, which, added to Persian adjectives, and in some few instances to Arabic, forms the comparative degree; as به good, بـﮩتر better; بد bad, بدتر worse; ضرور necessary, ضرورتر more necessary.
postpos. Under, beneath. See تلي tale.
subs: adj. Completely wet, saturated, drenched, soaking, dripping; covered (with blood)
s.m. A wet bandage (applied to a wound)
s.f. Application of a wet bandage (to a wound)
s.f. Activity, handiness, dexterity, expertness, adroitness
v.t. To wet; to steep, soak, to drench, saturate; — tar karne-wālā, adj. (in medicine) Demulcent
adj. Fresh and moist; ripe and fresh; quite fresh.

more: adj. 1. Intoxicated. 2. Inattentive, regardless, like one intoxicated.

ety: S. तार (for स्तार)+कं; and S. तारा fem.
def: s.m. A star; planet; falling star, meteor; the pupil of the eye
more: n. m. S. तारा 1. A star; planet; meteor ( shahāb-i-sāqib ). 2. The pupil; the apple of the eye. tārā bidyā ; A. ilm-i-hayat , n. m. Astronomy. tārā baras . Sidereal year. tārā ṭūṭnā , v. n. To fall (meteors from the sky). tārā din, yā kāl . Sidereal time. tārā ḍūbnā , v. n. To set (a star or planet). tārā manḍal , n. m. 1. The starry sphere. 2. A firework. tārā honā , v. n. 1. To be sky high; to touch the sky. 2. To appear as small as a star. tāroṅ bharī rāt , n. f. A starry night. tāroṅ kī chhāṅ , n. m. Dawn; day break. tāre dikhānā . A Mah. ceremony observed on the 6th day after childbirth. The lying-in-mother comes out with her child in her lap and looks towards the starry sky through a sieve, while two other women attend by her side with swords unsheathed. tāre dikhāī denā , v. n. To have lights dancing before one's eyes (from a bruise, etc.). tāre khil'nā, nikalnā, yā ugnā , v. n. T…

ety: inf. n. of طرب 'to be or become affected with emotion,' &c.
def: s.f. Emotion, joyous excitement, joy, mirth, cheerfulness, hilarity
more: s. f. Cheerfulness, mirth, merriment, hilarity, joy. طربگاه t̤arab-gāh, s. f. Place of merriment.

def: s.f. Haste, hurry, despatch, hurry-scurry, flight, panic; a number of deaths following in quick succession, great mortality
subs: v.n. A general flight to take place (in consequence of, -se); great mortality to occur.

def: n. f. 1. Haste; hurry; quickness. 2. An epidemic; great mortality. taṛā-bhaṛī paṛnā, yā honā , v. n. 1. To die like sheep. 2. To bolt or fly in great numbers; to be panic stricken. Āj kal haize se taṛā-bhaṛī ho rahī hai .

ety: inf. n. v of ردّ 'to cause to return,' &c.
def: s.m. Wavering, vacillation, fluctuation (of opinion), hesitation, indecision, irresolution, suspension of judgment; perplexity, anxious consideration, anxiety, trouble (of mind); refusal, rejection; debating; application, labour, exertion, endeavour, contrivance; cultivation, improvement
more: n. m. رد returning, causing to return. 1. Anxiety; concern; care ( udheṛ-bun , 1). 2. Cultivation; tillage.

(inf. v. of ردّ) s. m. 1. Irresolution, indecision, hesitation, fluctuation, suspension of judgment, wavering, anxious consideration. 2. Debating, rejecting, contrivance. ترددات دنيوي taraddudāt-i-dunyavī, Attention to worldly affairs.

def: s.m. Worldly engagements, attention to worldly concerns.

ety: inf. n. vi of ردف 'to ride behind'; 'to follow'
def: s.m. Following one another; succession, consecutiveness; synonymousness; a synonym; — adj. Consecutive, uninterrupted; carrying one behind another, carrying double (a horse).
more: (inf. vi. of ردف Heb. He followed) 1. s. m. Succession. 2. adj. Uninterrupted, carrying double (a horse).

def: n. f. Pl. atrāf , طرف looked towards. See اور 1—3. taraf sānī , n. m. The opposite or adverse party; the defendant. taraf-dār , n. A party-man ( pakshī , 2). taraf-dār , adj. Partial ( jānibdār ). taraf-dārī , n. f. Leaning; party-spirit; partizanship ( pās , 3). taraf-dārī k. , v. a. To side with ( jānib-dārī k .). taraf se . On the part of ( jānib se ). taraf h. , v. a. To be on one's side. apnī taraf dekhnā , v. n. To respect oneself; to have respect for one's own dignity, etc. Ajī! apnī taraf dekho! launḍe ke muṅh lagte ho! Dear Sir! consider your own dignity and lust not after the youth! apnī taraf se . 1. On one's side or part. Ham to apnī taraf se javāb de chuke . 2. From one's own pocket or private purse. Yeh inām apnī taraf se dīyā . bar-tarf , Dispensed with; dismissed; discarded. jiskī taraf se . On whose behalf. yak taraf , adv. Apart; separate. yak-tarfah . One-sided; ex-parte . yak-tarfah ḍigrī , n. f. An ex-parte decree.
more: (or t̤arf, from طرف To turn away, to strike the eye, compress the eye, wink) s. f. (dakh. s. m.) Side, margin, corner, quarter, extremity; towards. طرف ثاني t̤arf-i-s̤ānī, 1. A defendant. 2. An enemy. 3. ( met. ) A wife. طرفدار t̤araf-dār, 1. s. m. A partisan, a follower, a sectary, an assistant. 2. adj. Partial. طرفداري t̤araf-dār-ī, s. f. Following, partiality, assistance, &c.
wety: Ultimately from Arabic طَرَف (ṭaraf).

r: t̤araf, jānib, or, kī, t̤araf-ě-s̤ānī, pars altera, met., t̤araf-dār
ety: v.n. fr. طرف 'to look with the outer corner of the eye'
def: adj. & s.m. Taking a side; partial;-a party-man, partisan; a follower; a sectary; an assistant
subs: s.f. The act of taking a side, following, leaning, partiality, partisanship; party-spirit; favouring, assistance, &c.
(-kī), To take the side (of), to side (with), to show favour or partiality, to favour, &c.
(-kī, &c.), On the part (of), in behalf (of), from (e.g. merī t̤araf-se, 'in my behalf,' 'from me')
To regard oneself, to have respect for one's own dignity
On (his or my, &c.) side or part; from (his or my, &c.) own pocket, from (his or my, &c.) private purse
adv. Aside, apart, away, out of the way; out of the question; dismissed (from office)
(-ko), To put aside or away, to get rid of; to dismiss (from office)
adv. On one side, apart, separate.

def: s.m. = taḍāg, q.v. s.m. A pond, &c. ( = tālāb, q.v.). s.m. Pond, pool, tank, reservoir of water ( = talāʼo; cf. tāl).

ety: inf. n. v of رحم 'to have compassion'
def: s.m. Pity, compassion, mercy, kindness, lenity.
more: (inf. v. of رحم) s. m. Pity, compassion, kindness, mercy, lenity.

def: s.f. Lands lying at the foot of a watershed or on the banks of a river low ground flooded with water, valley, basin, marshy ground, marsh, swamp; meadow
more: n. f. A strip of land at the foot of a hill ( talaiṭī ).
subs: adj. & s.m. Living in or near water, aquatic, water-; water-fowl, water-spaniel, &c.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit *तलघट्टिका- (*talaghaṭṭikā-), ultimately derived from Proto-Indo-Aryan *taras (“plain, plateau”), possibly related to Sanskrit तल (tala, “level”); cognate to Nepali तराइ (tarāi). Possibly later associated with Classical Persian تر (tar, “humid”).

def: s.f. A star ( = taraiyā, tāra).
s. f. A marsh, mead, meadow. تراي کهيوا tarāʼī-khewā, adj. Living in, or near water, waterfowl, water-(spaniel, &c.).
s. m. Stars.
s.f. pl. (of taraʼī), Stars.
s.f. A star.
more: s. f. A woman, maid, female, wife. تريابيد tiriyā-bed ( स्त्री and वेद Knowledge) The science of women, knowledge of woman. ترياچرتر tiriyā-charitr ( स्त्रीचरित्र ) s.m. Female wiles. ترياراج tiriyā-rāj ( स्त्रीराज्य ) Amazon country, petticoat government.

def: s.m. Plunder, pillage, devastation (see tāḵẖt).
more: s. m. Plunder, devastation. تاراج کرنا tārāj karnā, To plunder, to spoil.

def: s.m. Deliverer, saviour, protector, preserver; — a star; the pupil of the eye.
more: s. m. 1. The crown of the head. 2. A hill, heap, head, top, summit.

def: s.m. A hill; heap; head, top, summit; the crown (of the head = tār, q.v.).
s.f. Rafter, beam.
more: E. n. f. A beam; rafter.
s. f. A beam, a rafter.
wety: From Classical Persian تُرک (turk), or conversely through Chagatai.

def: s.f. = taṛāk, q.v. s.m. Deliverer, saviour, protector, preserver; — a star; the pupil of the eye.
s.m. A pond, &c. ( = taḍāg), q.v. s.m. A pond, &c. ( = tālāb, q.v.). s.m. Pond, pool, tank, reservoir of water ( = talāʼo; cf. tāl).
s.f. Sound of the snapping, bursting, or cracking, &c. of a body or thing; sound of a stroke or blow; crack, crash, whack, &c. (see next)
subs: adv. In quick succession, quickly, rapidly; without delay or hesitation, readily, at once
adv. With a crack or crash, with a report; noisily, boisterously, rudely; without hesitation, smartly, quickly.

def: s.f. The Urdū form of the next, q.v.

ety: Prk. तडक्का from S. तट or तड+कृ
def: s.f. Cracking, snapping, &c.; crack, split, cranny, chink, cleft, crevice, fissure.
more: s. (dakh.) Crack, chap, split, cranny, cleft, chink, fissure, crevice ( Harris ): see تڙکنا taṛaknā.

def: s.m. Reprover, corrector, punisher, chastiser.
more: s. m. A reprover, punisher, chastiser.

def: s.f. The sound or noise of the snapping or splitting of a stone, &c. sound of a falling body, of a blow, &c.; crack ( = taṛāk, q.v.).
s.f. The sound or noise of the snapping or splitting of a stone, &c. sound of a falling body, of a blow, &c.; crack ( = taṛāk, q.v.).
more: n. S. तड The sound of beating; whack; crack; crash. taṛāk-paṛāk, taṛāq-paṛāq , adv. Rapidly; quickly; promptly; readily. Haṅsī ṭhaṭṭe, jugat, zile meṅ tāq, Chal rahī hai zabāṅ taṛāq-paṛāq . Wom. Shauq. In sprightly mirth and jests, bon mots , and repartee unmatched, Her fluent tongue from every flower of speech a grace still snatched. taṛāq se javāb denā , v. a. 1. To make a smart repartee. 2. To refuse sharply or rudely. taṛāq se ṭūṭnā , v. n. To break with a crackling noise.

def: s.m. = taṛākā, q.v. s.f. A star; falling star, meteor; the pupil of the eye.
more: s. m. (dakh.) Sound of striking, a blow.

def: s.m. Sound of snapping, &c. ( = taṛāk); pelting, down-pour (of a heavy shower); banging, pealing, rattling (of musketry, &c.); bustle, briskness, din, clamour (of an extensive business); clatter, clash, fury, violence (of a storm, &c.); sharpness, rigour, ardour, severity, intensity (of cold, heat, rain, wind, &c), pressure, scarcity, lack (of any commodity)
more: s. m. The sound of striking.
subs: adj. (f. ī), Pelting (as rain); violent (as a storm); intense (as cold, &c.); noisy, bustling, brisk (as a business); loud, gaudy; brilliant, splendid, magnificent.

def: adj. Pretty, showy, gaudy.

n. m. 1. Snap; crack; crackle; crash. 2. Smack (more com. chaṭ/?/khārā ); crack. Chakhte hī zabāṅ bhartī hai us ḍhab ke taṛāke, Shubrāt meṅ jis tarah se chhuṭte haiṅ, paṛāke . Rat-pickle. Nazīr. No sooner tasted than the lips smack smack, As crackers in Shabrāt go off crack crack. 3. Rage; force; violence; vehemence; severity. Kyā taṛāqe kā meṅh baras rahā hai . 4. Splendor; brilliancy; beauty. Gale kī safāī, woh kurtī kā chāk, Taṛāqe kī aṅgyā, kasī ṭhīk ṭhāk . Mīr Hasan. Her neck so smooth, her jacket's cut enough for zest, Her boddice' splendent sheen, tight fitting to her breast. 5. Lack; want; need; scarcity; drought. Nāj kā taṛākā bīt rahā hai .
s.f. A star; falling star, meteor; the pupil of the eye.
more: s. f. 1. The apple of the eye. 2. A star.

def: s.f. = taṛak, q.v. s.m. Deliverer, saviour, protector, preserver; — a star; the pupil of the eye.
more: s. (dakh.) Crack, chap, split, cranny, cleft, chink, fissure, crevice ( Harris ): see تڙکنا taṛaknā.

def: s. (dakh.) Root of the tongue. تارخ کوجيب لين لگانا tāraḵẖ ko jīb naiṅ lagānā, To chatter or talk much.
s.m. = taṛākā, q.v. s.f. A star; falling star, meteor; the pupil of the eye.
more: s. m. (dakh.) Crash, loud noise ( Harris ): see تڙاقا taṛāḳā.

def: v.n. = taṛaḵẖnā, taṛaknā, q.v.
more: v. n. (dakh.) To crack, to split, to warp (?): see تڙکنا taṛaknā.

def: v. n. (dakh.) To cleave ( Harris ): see تڙکنا taṛaknā.

ety: inf. n. v of رقي 'to ascend'
def: s.f. Rising step by step, advancement, elevation, promotion, preferment; progress, improvement, proficiency; increase
more: n. f. رقی he ascended. 1. Progress; improvement; advance; increase. 2. Advancement; promotion; preferment; elevation. taraq'qī pānā , v. n. To be promoted; to get advancement or promotion; to rise. taraq'qī denā yā karnā , v. a. 1. To advance; forward; push on. 2. To promote; raise; elevate.
(inf. v. of رقي) s. f. 1. Promotion, preferment, advancement, elevation. 2. Increase, improvement. 3. Making progress in learning, proficiency. (Plur. ترقيات Preferments, advances).
subs: v.n. To obtain preferment, be promoted, to rise; to be increased
(or, -meṅ) honā, To be progressing or improving; to be on the increase.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَرَقِّی (taraqqī), borrowed from Arabic تَرَقٍّ (taraqqin), from تَرَقَّى (taraqqā).

def: s.f. pl. (of taraqqī), Preferments, advances, &c.

ety: inf. n. v of رقب 'to watch for'
def: s.m. Waiting (for), watching (for); expectation; hoping; contemplating.
more: (inf. v. of رقب) s. m. Contemplating, expectation, hoping.

def: v. n. To be cracked, to be split.

ety: taṛak˚ = Prk. तडक्के(इ), fr. S. तड+कृ
def: v.n. To be cracked, split, &c.; to crack, split, burst, fly; break, snap asunder; to fly into a passion, become angry or enraged, to snap angrily or petulantly (at).
more: v. n. To be cracked, to be split, to burst, to crack.

def: s.f. The beam of a balance.
more: s. f. The beam of a balance.

def: s.f. = tarākṛī, q.v. s.f. The beam of a balance.

ety: inf. n. vi of رکم 'to heap up'
def: s.m. Being heaped together, being thickly collected together; accumulation, heap.
more: (inf. vi. of رکم) s. m. Heaping together, the being thickly collected together, accumulation.

def: adj. & s.m. Trembling, tremulous; unsteady, inconstant, changeable, fickle, capricious, volatile; ticklish; wanton, libidinous, lecherous; — splendid, glittering, sparkling, luminous; — a fickle person, &c.
more: adj. Capricious, changeable, fickle, inconstant, ticklish, volatile, unsteady.

def: s. (dakh.) Clap (noise), crashing noise ( Harris ).

s.m. A (wooden or other) building of a circular form with an arched or cupola roof; a wooden structure in gardens, &c. for the guarding of them; — a dome, vault; the vault of heaven ( = t̤āram-ě-ʻālạ̄).
more: (Pers. تارم) s. m. Any building of a circular form with an arched or cupola roof, a wooden structure in gardens, &c. for the guarding of them.

def: (dakh.) A sort of fireworks, like the دم نلا dam-nalā, q. v. under نلا nalā, but ever and anon stars burst up ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
s.m. Tax, impost, revenue, income.
more: s. m. 1. Safety. تران کرنا trāṅ karnā, To free, to protect. 2. A coat of mail.

def: s.m. Passing over, crossing; being saved, escape, deliverance; Svarga or paradise (the final landing-place); a raft; a boat; — one who is saved or delivered
subs: s.m. The saved and the saviour.

def: s. (dakh. for طرح t̤arḥ in the plur.) Modes, sorts.

adj. & s.m. Causing or enabling to cross; helping over a difficulty, &c.; liberating, saving; — who or what causes or enables to cross, one who delivers, deliverer, saviour; a raft, float; the bamboo framework of the roof of a house on which the thatch is laid; — crossing, passing over, reaching the opposite shore; salvation, deliverance
more: n. The bamboo frame of the roof of a house before the thatch is put on.
s. m. 1. The act of freeing, salvation, deliverance. 2. One that sets free or delivers.
subs: s.m. The saviour and the saved
adj. & s.m. Delivering, saving; — deliverer, saviour
v.t. To enable to cross; to save, &c. = tārnā, q.v.

def: s.m. Striking, beating, whipping, chastising; correction, chastisement, punishment; penalty; admonition, reproof, rebuke
more: (r. तड Beat) s. m. Punishment, admonition, reproof, penalty. تاڙناکرنا tāṛnā karnā, To reprove, punish, chastise.
subs: (or tāṛnā) karnā (-ko, or -kā), to reprove, rebuke, admonish; to correct, punish, &c.
v.t. To reprove, &c. ( = tāṛan karnā); to drive away, turn out.

def: s.m. Modulation, melody, harmony, symphony; voice, song, tune, air; trill, shake, quaver; a kind of song; an exercise in singing
more: s. m. 1. Modulation, harmony, voice, song, melody, symphony, trill, shake, quaver. 2. A kind of song. ترانه پرداز tarāna-pardāz, Composing songs.
subs: adj. & s.m. Composing melodies or songs; a composer of melodies, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَرَانَه (tarāna).

def: n. m. 1. Modulation; melody; harmony; symphony; a tune; an air. 2. An exercise in singing; a kind of song.
s.m. A raft or float (made of bamboos, &c. tied together, and sometimes floated upon jars or hollow gourds inverted); the float of a fishing line.
s.f. A raft, &c. ( = taranḍ); a boat, ship.
s.f. Wave, billow, ripple; emotion; rapture, transport, ecstasy; fancy, conceit, whim, caprice; becoming state and dignity.
more: n. f. 1. A wave. 2. See اُچنگ Kuchh āī jo us mah ke jī meṅ taraṅg, Kahā āj koṭhe pe bichchhe pilang! Mīr Hasan. 3. Transport; rapture; ecstasy. 4. Whim; fancy; conceit; caprice.
s. f. 1. Wave, emotion. 2. Whim, conceit, fancy, caprice. 3. Becoming state and dignity.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit तुरङ्ग (turaṅga).

def: s.m. An ornament for the ear ( = tāṭank, q.v.). s.m. An ornament for the ear, a large sort of ear-ring ( = tāṛaṅk).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀸𑀟 (tāḍa), from Sanskrit ताड (tāḍa).

def: adj. & s.m. Wavy, moving like a billow; moving restlessly to and fro, unsteady; fanciful, whimsical, capricious; fantastical; braggart; — an unsteady person; a whimsical person; a braggart.
s.f. A river.
part. adj. Wavy; overflowing (cf. tarangī).
s.m. = taranjubīn, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Passing through, pervading (space, &c.); — the sun; a ray of light.
more: s. f. A ship, a vessel, a boat.

def: s.f. A float, raft, boat; the plant Aloe perfoliata; the plant Hibiscus mutabilis.
s.f. A whip.
adj. Deserving of punishment, correction, admonition or reproof; punishable; reprovable.
s.m. An ornament for the ear ( = tāṭank, q.v.). s.m. An ornament for the ear, a large sort of ear-ring ( = tāṛaṅk).

ety: inf. n. v of رنم 'to trill or quaver'
def: s.m. Trilling, quavering, singing, modulating; song, modulation, rhythm.
more: (inf. v. of رنم) s. m. A song, modulation.

def: s.m. The ocean; a heavy shower, a torrent of rain; a fog.
s.f. A boat, vessel, ship ( = taraṇī).
adj. S. त्रिनवति Ninety-three.
s.f. = taṛap, q.v.
more: n. f. 1. Throb; flutter; palpitation. 2. A jump; leap; bound; spring. 3. A flash (of lightning).

ety: S. तट, or तड+स्फर; cf. the form taṛphaṛ-ānā
def: s.f. Tossing or rolling about restlessly or uneasily, feverish disquietude (of body or mind); agitation, flutter; throb, palpitation; anxious eagerness or desire; haste, hurry; leap, jump, bound, spring; glitter, flash (of lightning); crack, report, explosion; violence, fury, outrageousness.
more: (from تڙپنا) s. f. Haste, hurry, outrageousness, explosion, fury, leap, jump.

def: s.f. = taṛap, q.v.
(from تڙپهنا) s. f. Agitation, tossing about, palpitation.
s. f. See ٿڙپهـ taṛaph.

v.n. = taṛapnā, q.v. v.n. To roll or toss about restlessly or uneasily; to flounder, flounce; to be agitated; to flutter, beat, palpitate, throb; to quiver, vibrate, writhe, wriggle; to jump, spring, bound; to be anxiously eager (for), be eagerly desirous, to long (for).

v.n. = taṛapnā, q.v. v.n. To roll or toss about restlessly or uneasily; to flounder, flounce; to be agitated; to flutter, beat, palpitate, throb; to quiver, vibrate, writhe, wriggle; to jump, spring, bound; to be anxiously eager (for), be eagerly desirous, to long (for).
v. n. See ٿڙپنا taṛapnā.

v.n. To roll or toss about restlessly or uneasily; to flounder, flounce; to be agitated; to flutter, beat, palpitate, throb; to quiver, vibrate, writhe, wriggle; to jump, spring, bound; to be anxiously eager (for), be eagerly desirous, to long (for).
more: taṛaphnā, v. n. 1. To flutter, palpitate, flounce, be agitated, writhe, jump, spring, bound, wriggle, toss. 2. To be very desirous about any thing.
taṛaphnā, v. n. To quiver, vibrate.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀢𑀟𑀧𑁆𑀨𑀟𑀇 (taḍapphaḍaï).

def: n. f. Hesitation; delay.
s.m. = taṛeṛā, q.v.
more: s. m. Throwing water. See تريڙا.

def: v. n. (dakh.) To rear (as, a horse).
s.m. (local), A hyena.

ety: Zend thwareś, fr. thwakhsh = S. त्वक्ष्
def: s.f. Cutting, hewing, paring, clipping; shaving; cut, shape, form, fashion (of clothes, &c.), make, build; — part. adj. & s.m. Cutting, chiselling, fashioning, &c.; — cutter, carver, fashioner, &c. (used as last member of compounds, e.g. but-tarāsh, 'Image-carver')
more: n. f. تراشيدن to shave; pare. Cut; shape; form; fashion (of clothes); make; mould; build.
(from تراشيدن) 1. part. in compos. Cutting; cutter, carver. 2. s. f. Cutting, cut, paring, shape, form, fashion (of clothes, &c.). تراش ڐالنا tarāsh ḍālnā, To pare off or away.
subs: s.f. Shaping and fashioning; arranging (dress, &c.) neatly or elegantly; elegance of dress, &c.; fine or neat shape or form; — scratching out, erasure; — tarāsh-ḵẖarāsh-karnā, v.t. To make (oneself, apne taʼīṅ) spruce, to dress or attire oneself neatly or elegantly; — to scratch out, erase (writing, a word, &c.).

def: n. f. 1. Erasures; alterations; corrections ( chhīl chhāl ). 2. Fine shape or form; elegance in dress, coiffure, etc. tarāsh kharāsh karnā , v. a. 1. To scratch out; erase; alter; amend. 2. To get oneself up very smart.
more: s. f. Shape (particularly, good), neatness of form.

ety: Zend thwarsta; S. त्वष्ट; see tarāsh
def: s.m. lit. 'What is pared off,' &c.; paring, shaving, chip, splinter, clip, shred, scrap.
more: s. m. Shaving, paring, splinter, chip, shred, scrap.

def: v.t. To cut, hew, pare, clip, prune; to cut out, carve, shape, form, fashion; to shave (the beard, &c.)
more: v. a. 1. To cut; clip; slice; prune. 2. To cut out; carve; shape. 3. To coin or use (big words). Lugat tarāshnā. apne taīṅ tarāshnā , v. n. 1. To be a swell. 2. To value or esteem one's self above others.
(from تراش) v. a. 1. To cut, to clip, prune. 2. To shave. 3. To shape, to cut out. اپني تئين تراشنا apne taʼīṅ tarāshnā, To be vain, to value or esteem oneʼs self above others.
subs: To make oneself spruce, (vulg.) to come out a swell; to value or esteem oneself above others
v.t. (intens. of tarāshnā), To cut up, to pare off, pare away, &c. ( = ćhīl-ḍālnā).
